subject: [PATCH v2 8/9] mm: percpu: skip the per-cpu node walk on single-node systems

2026-06-26.patch_summary: This patch optimizes the per-cpu memory allocation by skipping a time-consuming walk of possible CPU nodes on single-node systems. The existing code would perform this walk to determine the NUMA node for each backing page, but since all pages are already on the same node in a single-node system, this step is unnecessary and only adds overhead. A new fast path has been added to pcpu_memcg_accumulate() that directly attributes the allocation to the first online node when there's only one online node, thus avoiding the O(num_possible_cpus) walk.
